question_text: Which of the following best describes a non-contact force?

options.label: A
options.text: A force that only acts when two surfaces are pressed together

options.label: B
options.text: A force that can act between objects separated by a distance

options.label: C
options.text: A force that is always weaker than contact forces

options.label: D
options.text: A force that only occurs in liquids and gases

correct_answer: A force that can act between objects separated by a distance
explanation: Non-contact forces are forces that can act on objects without them touching. Examples include gravity (which pulls objects together), magnetic forces (which can push or pull without contact), and electrostatic forces (between charged objects). These forces work across a distance, unlike contact forces like friction or normal forces that require surfaces to touch.
